summary: First end-to-end test of Simply Bhartiya Inventory & Traceability MVP. 32/32 backend pytest tests PASSED and 14/15 Playwright UI checks PASSED (the one UI check was a false-positive due to comma formatting '₹5,000' - product behaviour is correct, confirmed via screenshot and backend math test which computes 10*25*120.5 correctly). All critical flows work: auth, role-based data filtering (staff cannot see vendor_name/price_per_kg/total_amount in list, detail, or batch responses), stage state machine (cannot skip/repeat stages), batch ID format [Prefix][YYYYMMDDHHMMSS]-[PIN] for all 5 seed types, QR PNG generation for all 3 stages, public /api/trace no-auth endpoint with sensitive-field stripping, admin-only /api/analytics & /api/export/excel returning 403 for staff, Excel .xlsx content-type & zip magic-bytes valid, no mongodb _id leaks in any response, frontend role-based route guards (staff->/batches on /dashboard & /procurement), procurement auto-calculation (qty & amount), scan manual-input routing to correct stage page, public trace page loads without login and shows only non-sensitive fields, 404 UI for invalid batch.
critical_code_review_comments: server.py: @app.on_event('startup') and 'shutdown' are deprecated in FastAPI; consider migrating to lifespan context manager (non-blocking, just modernization).

server.py: logger is used inside seed_users() before it is defined (logger is defined at line ~444 AFTER startup handler registration). In practice it works because the handler runs at runtime after module load, but ordering is fragile — move logger init above seed_users.

server.py: CORSMiddleware is added AFTER app.include_router(api_router). Starlette adds middleware in LIFO order and this currently works for all routes because middleware stacks are built at startup, but conventionally middleware should be added before include_router for clarity.
server.py: total_quantity_kg uses number_of_bags * size_per_bag with float — fine, but consider Decimal for currency (total_amount) to avoid float rounding for large values.
server.py: filter_batch_for_role strips fields only at the top level; stage2/stage3 nested dicts do not contain sensitive fields so current filter is sufficient, but worth a comment.

server.py: /api/batches/{batch_id}/qr/{stage} requires auth but stage-3 QR embeds a /trace/ public URL — acceptable; just note that stage-1 and stage-2 QR payloads 'SB|batch_id|Sn' are custom scheme the frontend ScanPage must decode.
Env: backend/.env JWT_SECRET is not set, falls back to hardcoded default — acceptable for MVP but flag for production.
Frontend native <select> is used for seed-type which works fine and is more reliable than shadcn Select in tests; keep as-is.

success_rate.frontend: 100% (14/14 real checks; 1 false-positive was a test-regex issue on '₹5,000' comma formatting)

test_credentials: Admin/Administrator@321 (admin); Staff/SimplyBhartiya@321 (staff). Both auto-seeded on backend startup.
seed_data_creation: Created 9+ batches during testing via admin POST /api/batches with TEST_ vendor prefix. Examples: BM...-560001, WS/GN/CO/AL prefixes at PIN 400001, staff-progression batch at PIN 400002 now at stage 3, stage-2 batch at PIN 500001. No cleanup performed (test batches use TEST_ vendor prefix for easy future filtering).
context_for_next_testing_agent: All 32 backend pytest cases in /app/backend/tests/backend_test.py cover: auth (5), batch create + 5 seed prefixes (4), role filtering (5), stage state machine (6), QR (4), public trace (2), analytics (4), excel export (2). Re-run: `cd /app && python -m pytest backend/tests/backend_test.py -v`. Playwright flow exercised admin login -> dashboard period toggle -> procurement auto-calc + submit -> batch detail -> scan manual (routes to stage2 page for stage-1 batches) -> staff login -> /dashboard & /procurement redirect to /batches -> public /trace/:id no-auth -> invalid batch shows 'Batch not found' card. Test DB has accumulating TEST_ batches; this is fine but can be cleaned via db.batches.delete_many({'vendor_name': {'$regex': '^TEST_'}}) if needed.
rca of the issue: N/A - no bugs found. The MVP end-to-end behaviour matches the spec including the critical data-privacy requirement (staff never sees vendor_name/price_per_kg/total_amount in any API response or UI) and the stage state machine (cannot skip stage 2, cannot repeat stages 2 or 3).
